summary: Tight '81 show with scorching Eyes Of The World and jam‑filled second set
blurb: The band delivers a tight performance highlighted by an incendiary Eyes Of The World and a seamless Uncle John's Band → Playing In The Band → China Doll sequence. Jerry’s crystal‑clear guitar and Brent’s liquid keyboards shine, while the drum jam propels a high‑energy second set. A rare Casey Jones encore caps a fun summer night.
review: July 10 1981 at the St. Paul Civic Center finds the Dead in top form. From the opening “Jack Straw” through a breezy first set, Jerry’s tone is described as “crystal‑clear” and his vocals “clean my clock,” while Brent’s early‑'80 keyboards add a liquid sheen that lifts songs like “Sugaree.” The second set ignites with an “Estimated Prophet → Eyes Of The World” stretch that many reviewers call scorching, leading into a drum jam that injects fresh surprise. From there the band weaves through an immaculate Uncle John's Band → Playing In The Band → China Doll sandwich and a fiery “Around & Around,” keeping the crowd on its feet. Bob’s segues are noted as hot, even a “hump” into “Around & Around” that fuels the groove. The night closes with a rare encore of “Casey Jones,” making this summer 1981 outing both fun and historically satisfying. Across soundboard and audience tapes the show is praised for its balanced mix, crisp guitar, tight pacing, and an overall vibe that feels like pure gravy after the Playing In The Band reprise.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All five recordings are marked positive, repeatedly praising the Eyes Of The World performance, drum jam, and seamless second‑set segues. Consistent language such as “scorching,” “crystal‑clear,” and “pure gravy” across multiple sources signals strong positive consensus.

best_recording.reason: Soundboard capture with balanced, crystal‑clear mix; minimal crowd bleed and clean guitar make it the most faithful listening experience.

key_highlights: Scorching Eyes Of The World that drives the second set
Jam‑filled drum segment energizing the flow into Space
Uncle John's Band → Playing In The Band → China Doll sandwich as pure gravy
Rare Casey Jones encore capping a fun summer night

band_performance.Jerry: Crystal‑clear guitar tone and crisp vocals shine throughout, especially on Eyes Of The World and the second‑set jams.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s segues are hot—his “hump” into Around & Around is praised—and his attitude snarls add grit to the set.
band_performance.Brent: His early‑'80 keyboard work is described as liquid and solid, providing bright color despite a few first‑set hiccups.
band_performance.Mickey: Drum solo creates an energizing catalyst for the second set, delivering a surprise jam that lifts the whole band.
band_performance.Billy: Alongside Mickey, delivers a flawless drum segment that propels the show’s momentum.
